stories.headline: [Health-e News] Climate Change Scuppers Efforts To End Malaria In SADC The recent heavy rains and associated flooding further exacerbated malaria efforts in several countries recovering from the 2025 upsurges.
stories.summary: Heavy rains and flooding in Southern Africa have exacerbated malaria efforts, particularly in countries recovering from the 2025 upsurges. Climate change is a growing threat to malaria programmes, as increased rainfall and warmer temperatures create ideal breeding conditions for malaria vectors. Stagnant floodwaters have become breeding sites for Anopheles mosquitoes, increasing the risk of malaria transmission. New outbreaks have been reported in Botswana, Eswatini, Namibia, and Zimbabwe. After two decades of progress, the fight against malaria has stalled due to climate change and population growth. Health experts warn that early awareness is crucial to saving lives, as the region faces a worrying resurgence of the potentially fatal disease. The Southern African Development Community (SADC) region is struggling to end malaria due to these challenges.

stories.headline: [BBC] US says 'no time frame' on ending war with Iran, as Tehran says reopening Strait of Hormuz 'not possible' The White House says Donald Trump is "satisfied" with the blockade of Iran's ports, which Tehran's top negotiator calls a violation of the ceasefire.
stories.summary: The US has stated there is "no time frame" for ending the conflict with Iran. President Donald Trump expressed satisfaction with the blockade of Iran's ports, which Tehran's top negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, calls a violation of the ceasefire. Ghalibaf said reopening the Strait of Hormuz is "not possible" due to US naval blockades and "warmongering" by Israel. Trump announced the US would extend the ceasefire until Iran's leaders come up with a "unified proposal" to US negotiating positions. He had earlier threatened to renew bombing if a deal was not reached. The US and Iran remain at odds, with Tehran citing "blatant violations" of the ceasefire and the US maintaining its blockade. The situation remains unresolved, with no clear end in sight to the conflict. Trump's approach has been influenced by political considerations, according to some, but he denies this.

stories.headline: [The Times of Israel] Body of journalist for pro-Hezbollah daily pulled from rubble of home struck by IDF Military says it targeted building ‘to which terrorists fled’ after striking one of their cars in Israeli-held security zone; photographer accompanying Amal Kha
stories.summary: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) struck a building in southern Lebanon, killing a journalist, Amal Khalil, who worked for the pro-Hezbollah daily Al-Akhbar. The IDF said it targeted the building after militants fled there following a strike on one of their cars in the Israeli-held security zone. The military claimed the vehicle approached troops in a manner posing an immediate threat to their safety. Lebanese rescue teams recovered Khalil's body from the rubble. The IDF stated that the details regarding journalists being hit were "under review." The incident occurred after the IDF struck a vehicle in the security zone, targeting what they said was a car used by militants. The military is investigating the incident, which also reportedly wounded two journalists. The strike was part of an ongoing exchange of attacks in the region.

stories.headline: [Business Insider] Russians Turn Recon Drone Into Mothership for FPV Quadcopters Russia has used loitering munitions as drone carriers before, but this time used an Orlan-10 recon drone, which has a range of about 74 miles.
stories.summary: Russia has modified its Orlan-10 reconnaissance drone to carry smaller FPV quadcopters, increasing the range of drone strikes. The Orlan-10 has a range of approximately 74 miles and is a reusable platform. This is not the first time Russia has used drones as carriers, having previously used loitering munitions like the Shahed exploding drone and Gerbera decoy drone. However, the Orlan-10's reusability sets it apart. A Russian defense industrial base source confirmed that Orlan-10 UAVs are being used to transport loitering munitions, significantly increasing the range of Russian drone strikes. The modification allows Russia to launch FPV drones from a distance, potentially expanding its drone capabilities. The use of the Orlan-10 as a mothership was spotted in recent footage, with one instance showing the drone dropping an FPV quadcopter before being downed by Ukrainian forces.

stories.headline: Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses review: Strong audio and camera undermined by unreliable AI
stories.summary: The Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses offer strong audio and camera capabilities, but their AI features are inconsistent and limited in real-world use. The glasses have a sleek design, a useful built-in display, and responsive gesture control. However, they are heavy, have poor battery life, and lack third-party app support, making them a pricey option. Despite these drawbacks, the Ray-Ban Meta (gen 2) smart glasses are a modest upgrade over the previous generation and remain a top pick due to the lack of competition. The glasses feature a 12MP camera that can take still images and video, and their Meta AI functionality is surprisingly fun and handy, making them a solid choice for those looking for an AI wearable. Overall, the glasses are a game-changer, but their high price and limitations may deter some buyers.

stories.headline: Crimson Desert Patch 1.04.0 Adds Game Difficulty Settings, New Storage Items, and Pets
stories.summary: Crimson Desert's patch 1.04.0 has been released, introducing game difficulty settings, new storage items, and pets. The update also includes keyboard/mouse and controller preset options, new category tabs for the inventory, and new skills. Additionally, the patch improves distant scenery quality and allows players to change the names of pets and horses. Five new types of cat pets have been added, and the Abyss Heuklang can now be petted. The 37GB PC patch is part of Pearl Abyss' ongoing efforts to enhance the game, which has surpassed five million sales in under a month. The update aims to provide a better gaming experience for players, with reworked boss combat and other improvements. The full patch notes are available, detailing all the changes and additions made to the game.

stories.headline: Stuff Your Kindle Day is live now - score 150+ free ebooks for 1 day only
stories.summary: Stuff Your Kindle Day is a one-day event where hundreds of authors offer their e-books for free on online retailers. The latest event is live, offering over 150 free e-books for a limited time. The Cozy Mystery Book Blast, hosted by Cozy Mystery Book Club, is providing cozy mystery books for free, while Stuff Your E-Reader, hosted by Indie Author Collective, is offering books in various genres for Kindle and Kobo. The purpose of Stuff Your Kindle Day is to discover new authors and read on a budget. Participating authors offer their e-books for free, allowing readers to explore new genres and titles without cost. The event is held periodically, with the next one scheduled for April 23, 2026. Readers can take advantage of the free e-books by visiting the participating online retailers.

stories.headline: Late-Night Snacks Likely Disrupt Your Gut Health
stories.summary: Researchers analyzed data from over 11,000 participants in the US National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey to study the link between chronic stress, late-night eating, and bowel dysfunction. The study found that chronic stress disrupts bowel functions and is worsened by late-night eating habits, leading to a significant decrease in gut microbiome diversity. Participants who ate late at night were 2.5 times more likely to report bowel problems. The combination of high stress and late eating was found to have a detrimental effect on digestive health. To mitigate this, small changes such as eating meals at regular times, reducing heavy late-night meals, and managing stress can be beneficial. The study highlights the importance of maintaining a healthy lifestyle to protect gut health, particularly for individuals who engage in late-night snacking as part of their busy lifestyle.

stories.headline: Lamine Yamal injury update - Will Barcelona and Spain star be able to play in FIFA World Cup 2026
stories.summary: Barcelona's Lamine Yamal will miss the rest of the season with a hamstring injury, but is expected to be available for Spain at the 2026 World Cup. The injury occurred during a match against Celta Vigo on Wednesday, with Yamal suffering a suspected hamstring tear. According to Barcelona, Yamal will be out for the remainder of the domestic season, but is anticipated to recover in time for the World Cup. The severity of the injury is still being assessed, with possible recovery scenarios ranging from 2-3 weeks for a Grade 1 strain to 3-8 weeks for a partial tear. Teammate Pedri expressed hope that Yamal's recovery would be swift, wishing him the best of luck. Despite the setback, Yamal's club is confident he will be ready to represent Spain at the World Cup.

stories.headline: BVB, News and Rumours: Niko Kovac must meet two clear targets in the final stretch of the season
stories.summary: Borussia Dortmund's season is underperforming, causing concern among fans and management. Manager Niko Kovac is reportedly being given two clear targets to meet in the final stretch of the season. Kovac's future at the club is uncertain, with rumors suggesting a potential departure if targets are not met. Meanwhile, Kovac has been linked to Liverpool as a potential successor to Jurgen Klopp, with the Daily Mail reporting that he is being considered for the role. Kovac is currently a free agent after being sacked by Wolfsburg in March. Despite this, he has spoken out about his frustrations with modern football, citing issues that bother him. The club's history of success, including national titles in 1956 and 1957, adds pressure to the current situation. Kovac's performance will be closely watched as the season concludes.

stories.headline: How Social Media Reacted to the OKC Thunder Beating the Suns in Game 2
stories.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Phoenix Suns in Game 2 of the 2026 NBA Playoffs, taking a 2-0 lead in the series. The Thunder's win sparked a reaction on social media, with fans and analysts praising the team's performance. Chet Holmgren's presence was noted as a key factor in the team's success, with some suggesting it's no coincidence that the Thunder became the 1st seed in the West since he started playing. Jalen Williams, who was limited to 33 games in the regular season due to injuries, is still finding his rhythm. The Suns, meanwhile, are looking to reset the series' tone after a lopsided loss in Game 1, with guard Jalen Green citing pace as a key factor. The Thunder's dominant performance has set the tone for the series, with fans and analysts eagerly awaiting the next game.

stories.headline: Magic Millions Release World Class Catalogue for National Broodmare Sale
stories.summary: Magic Millions has released a world-class catalogue for the 2026 National Broodmare Sale, featuring seven Group One winners and 111 black-type performers. The sale will take place on the Gold Coast next month, offering 524 quality lots. The catalogue includes elite racetrack performers such as Startantes, Legarto, O' Ole, Stretan Angel, and Vibrant Sun. Startantes will be offered in foal to Australian Champion Anamoe. The sale is expected to be Australia's biggest and most commercial of its kind, with a deep and impressive lineup of race fillies and broodmares. Magic Millions Managing Director Barry Bowditch has described the catalogue as "world class", highlighting its depth and quality. The National Broodmare Sale has a strong reputation, and this year's catalogue is set to attract significant interest from buyers.

stories.headline: Tamil Nadu elections 2026 | Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan, Ajith Kumar and others cast their votes
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu's Legislative Assembly elections were held on April 23, 2026, with voting taking place across 234 constituencies. Notable figures, including Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan, and Ajith Kumar, cast their votes. The state has an electorate of 5.73 crore, with 75,064 polling stations set up to decide the fate of 4,023 candidates. Voting concluded at 6 PM, with voters in queues allowed to cast their ballots beyond the deadline. The election results will be declared on May 4, 2026, after the votes are counted. The Tamil Nadu elections are being closely watched, with various alliances and candidates vying for power in the state. The voter turnout and constituency-wise updates are being tracked, with an interactive data dashboard available to explore alliances, candidates, and elector statistics.

stories.headline: West Bengal Election 2026 LIVE: Voting underway in 1st phase; Several injured in crude bomb attack in Murshidabad
stories.summary: Voting is underway in the first phase of the West Bengal Assembly Election 2026, with 152 out of 294 constituencies going to the polls. The voting covers eight North Bengal districts and parts of Murshidabad, Nadia, Birbhum, and Hooghly. A crude bomb attack in Murshidabad's Nowda area has left several people injured. Despite incidents of violence, a high voter turnout of 91.58% was recorded in the first phase. Clashes were reported in Murshidabad, with voters in Domkal alleging intimidation and threats. As of 11 a.m., the state had registered a voting percentage of 41.11%. The election has seen a significant turnout, with voters casting their ballots across 16 districts of West Bengal. The first phase of voting took place on Thursday, April 23.

stories.headline: Poll violence in West Bengal: Crude bombs hurled in Murshidabad, several injured
stories.summary: Crude bombs were hurled in Naoda, Murshidabad district, West Bengal, injuring several people, including a woman, ahead of and during polling in the state assembly elections. The incident triggered panic and tension in the area. Unidentified individuals allegedly threw the bombs near a voting area, sparking clashes and allegations of booth capturing by TMC workers in the Domkal area. Opposition cadres were reportedly targeted. The explosions occurred late on Tuesday night and on Thursday, with multiple people injured. The situation remains tense, with more details awaited regarding the incidents. Polling is ongoing in the state, with reports of clashes and allegations of violence emerging from various areas, including Murshidabad district.

stories.headline: Karnataka SSLC Result 2026: KSEAB 10th result today - Check step to download marks memo at karresults.nic.in
stories.summary: The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) has declared the Karnataka SSLC Results 2026. The results were released on April 23, 2026, at 12:00 PM on the official website karresults.nic.in. Students can access their marks card by using their registration number and date of birth. This year, 7 students have achieved a perfect score of 625 out of 625 marks. Due to potential website delays, candidates can also use DigiLocker or SMS services to access their results quickly. The official result link is available on karresults.nic.in, and students can check and download their marksheets using their login details.

stories.headline: Indian markets open lower as crude, geopolitics weigh; all eyes on Infosys Q4 earnings
stories.summary: Indian markets opened lower on Thursday, with the Nifty 50 slipping 170 points to 24,200.70 and the BSE Sensex shedding over 600 points to 77,850. Elevated crude oil prices and renewed US-Iran tensions weighed on investor sentiment. Most sectoral indices opened lower, tracking a cautious mood across Asian peers. Early gains were seen after South Korea's economy grew 1.7% in the January-March quarter, its fastest pace since December 2020. Infosys is set to announce its March quarter earnings later in the day, which will be closely watched for direction. The earnings report is expected to provide insight into the company's performance and guidance, influencing the market's trajectory. The Indian stock market's performance will be closely monitored as investors await the release of Infosys' Q4 earnings and other key results.

stories.headline: Bengal election Bangladesh relations Campaign rhetoric on migration, minority politics and border issues raises concerns in Bangladesh while Teesta deadlock and shifting geopolitics test bilateral
stories.summary: The West Bengal election has raised concerns in Bangladesh due to campaign rhetoric on migration, minority politics, and border issues. The Teesta water-sharing agreement deadlock and shifting geopolitics are testing bilateral relations between India and Bangladesh. Bangladesh has invested in building a cooperative relationship with India, but subnational dynamics, such as state-level politics in West Bengal, are affecting the relationship. The BJP's references to Bangladesh have been used to frame West Bengal as a frontier in a national project of border control and cultural consolidation, while the TMC has recast this rhetoric as an external imposition. The election has also highlighted the vulnerability of the TMC's Muslim voter support base and the potential for anxiety among Hindu voters in border-adjacent districts. The unresolved Teesta agreement shows how domestic politics can shape critical bilateral relations. Bangladesh is closely watching the election's impact on India-Bangladesh ties.

stories.headline: Global Inc speaks out: Iran war hurt biz already bruised by tariffs, high costs
stories.summary: Companies across various sectors, including consumer goods, travel, and mining, are warning that the US-Iran war is increasing costs, disrupting supply chains, and hurting consumer confidence. The conflict is exacerbating existing pressures from US tariffs, higher input costs, and weak demand. The global economy is facing strains from the energy shock, with factories struggling with soaring production costs and weakening activity. The war has also raised concerns about stagflation, a combination of stagnant economic growth and high inflation. Business surveys have shown that the surge in energy prices and rising uncertainty are dampening economic activity, with major economies around the world already feeling the toll. The upcoming business surveys from the US to the euro zone will provide a collective health check of the global economy since the war broke out.

stories.headline: Iran war hurt biz already bruised by tariffs, high costs Investors and economists are watching to see whether companies can absorb the shock, or if prolonged uncertainty over energy, transport and geopolitics forces m
stories.summary: The US-Israeli war with Iran is driving up costs and disrupting supply chains for companies across various sectors, including consumer goods, travel, and mining. The conflict has led to increased transport and raw material costs, particularly due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This has resulted in reduced visibility and clouded financial outlooks for many companies. Despite some companies maintaining their full-year forecasts, executives have flagged rising costs and uncertainty as major concerns. The conflict has also impacted global energy markets, with Canadian oil producers seeing high profits as buyers seek alternatives. The Canadian government has introduced measures such as suspending federal taxes on fuels and implementing food subsidies to mitigate the effects of the crisis. The uncertainty surrounding the conflict is expected to impact 2026 growth outlooks in Canada and the US.

stories.headline: Pakistan Gambled on Mediation As U.S.-Iran talks falter, the political and economic risks are rising for Islamabad.
stories.summary: Pakistan's mediation efforts between the US and Iran have stalled, with the second round of talks in Islamabad delayed after Iran seized two cargo vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The collapse of peace talks poses significant political and economic risks for Pakistan, which has publicly invested in mediation. If talks fail, Islamabad may be seen as overpromising and underdelivering, damaging its reputation. Pakistan's role as a mediator has its own pitfalls, and the country could become the "fall guy" if the war resumes. Security analyst Muhammad Faisal notes that Pakistan has invested political capital in mediation, and failure could have negative consequences. The delayed talks have cast doubt on the future of US-Iran negotiations, with Pakistan's gamble on mediation potentially backfiring. The situation remains uncertain, with markets and international diplomatic relations at risk.

stories.headline: US and China Trade Talks Stall Over Tariff Dispute
stories.summary: US and China trade talks have stalled due to a tariff dispute. Despite this, the US has cut tariffs on certain Chinese goods from 20% to 10%, with President Trump stating that China will purchase large amounts of soybeans and other farm products in return. The US had previously imposed a 25% tariff on Mexico and Canada, but has since cut tariffs on Chinese imports from 145% to a range of 10-30%. China has also reduced levies on US imports from 125% to 10%. The development has eased trade tensions, causing US financial markets to rally, with the S&P 500 surging 7.5%. President Trump has expressed optimism about reaching a "fair" trade deal with China, although no further details have been disclosed. The trade talks are ongoing, with the US pausing higher tariffs on all countries except China.

stories.headline: European Union Leaders Meet to Discuss Brexit Deadline
stories.summary: European Union leaders are meeting to discuss the Brexit deadline and a potential "reset" of the UK-EU relationship. The UK voted to leave the EU in 2016, and since then, negotiations have been ongoing. The EU had previously regretted the UK's decision to drop the defence and security chapter during Brexit talks. Recently, the Commission sought to open negotiations on a youth mobility scheme. The UK and EU have agreed to a landmark deal aimed at easing restrictions on travel and work for millions of people. The deal is expected to include a security pact, a declaration on global issues, and a common understanding of future topics to be negotiated, with potential discussions on defence, youth mobility, and £25bn in trade. The meeting aims to rebuild Britain's relationship with the EU and achieve key agreements.

stories.headline: North Korea Fires Missile Into Sea of Japan Amid Tensions
stories.summary: North Korea launched multiple unidentified ballistic missiles toward the Sea of Japan on April 19, 2026, detected by South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff around 6:10 a.m. local time. The missiles were fired from the Sinpo area on North Korea's eastern coast. This incident marks the latest in a series of provocative weapons tests by North Korea, which has conducted several launches since the start of 2026. The country had previously fired around 10 ballistic missiles into the sea on March 14, 2026, amid US-South Korea military drills. The launches are seen as a show of force by North Korea, which has been increasing tensions in the region with its repeated missile tests. The actions come despite expressions of openness to renewed dialogue with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

stories.headline: Indian stock markets experienced a second consecutive day of losses, with the Sensex tumbling 852 points, as crude oil prices surpassed USD 100 per barrel due t
stories.summary: Indian stock markets recorded significant losses for the second consecutive day, with the Sensex tumbling 852.49 points. The decline was driven by crude oil prices breaching the $100 per barrel mark, with Brent crude trading at $103.8 per barrel. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and stalled US-Iran negotiations contributed to the surge in oil prices. The Nifty50 also fell, dropping below 24,250. The losses came after a previous day of gains, where the Sensex had jumped over 760 points and the Nifty had neared 24,600. The current decline marks a reversal in the market's trend, with investors closely watching the impact of rising oil prices on the economy. The BSE Sensex closed at a lower level, reflecting the market's concerns over the ongoing geopolitical tensions and their effect on crude oil prices.

stories.headline: The Indian stock market opened in the red on Thursday, tracking a cautious mood across Asian peers as elevated crude oil prices and renewed U.S.-Iran tensions d
stories.summary: The Indian stock market opened lower on Thursday, with the Nifty 50 slipping 170 points to 24,200.70, a 0.7% decline, and the BSE Sensex shedding over 600 points to around 77,850. Elevated crude oil prices and renewed US-Iran tensions dampened investor sentiment, leading to a cautious mood across Asian markets. This follows a sharp rally on Wednesday, where the Sensex and Nifty rose over 1.6% each, driven by easing oil prices and hopes of US-Iran peace talks. The decline on Thursday indicates a reversal in market sentiment, with most sectoral indices also trading in the red. The market is closely watching global developments and their impact on oil prices and geopolitical tensions.

stories.headline: Indian equity benchmarks declined for a second straight session, pressured by a spike in crude oil prices, a weakening rupee and rising geopolitical tensions. B
stories.summary: Indian equity benchmarks declined for a second straight session, pressured by a spike in crude oil prices, a weakening rupee, and rising geopolitical tensions. The Sensex plummeted 721.08 points to 81,463.09, while the Nifty fell 225.10 points to 24,837. Broad-based selling, FII outflows, and rising bond yields weighed on sentiment, increasing volatility. The rupee hit a new all-time low due to weak foreign flows and uncertainty surrounding trade negotiations with the US. Disappointing earnings also contributed to the decline. Analysts expect near-term caution ahead of a heavy IPO calendar and the RBI's upcoming decisions. The decline marked the fifth straight weekly loss for Indian benchmark shares, their longest losing streak in about eight months.

stories.headline: The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex traded lower in the morning session as oil prices extended gains following fresh tensions in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex traded lower in morning sessions due to extended gains in oil prices, sparked by fresh tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. The Indian markets opened sharply lower, with Sensex falling over 680 points and Nifty slipping below 24,250. This decline is attributed to the escalation of the Iran-US conflict, crude oil prices surpassing $100, and persistent selling pressure. The tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have led to a rise in oil prices, impacting the Indian stock markets. As a result, both Nifty50 and BSE Sensex have been trading in the red, with Nifty50 below 24,200 and BSE Sensex dropping significantly. The Indian stock markets have been closely watching global cues, which have turned mixed, further affecting the market performance.

stories.headline: HSBC downgrades India to 'underweight' as oil clouds outlook
stories.summary: HSBC has downgraded Indian stocks to "underweight" due to rising energy prices from the Middle East war, which could impact India's earnings recovery. The market now looks less attractive compared to North East Asian peers. Foreign investors have been selling Indian stocks, and the country's crude oil imports sank by 13% in March from February as the Iran war crippled Middle Eastern supply. This is the second downgrade in less than a month, with HSBC previously downgrading Indian equities to "neutral" from "overweight" on March 31. The brokerage firm cites $100 oil and the West Asia war as factors clouding earnings. HSBC Private Bank has also slashed India stock exposure to buy gold amid the uncertainty. The downgrade reflects concerns over India's vulnerability to external shocks, particularly rising energy prices.

stories.headline: SoftBank Group Shares Rally as AI Stocks Lead Market Recovery
stories.summary: SoftBank Group's shares rallied, rising as much as 8.9 percent, as artificial-intelligence-related stocks led a market recovery. The surge in SoftBank's shares reflects investor enthusiasm for its expanding role in artificial intelligence investments. The company's shares had previously tumbled over 13 percent on the Tokyo Stock Exchange due to a broader sell-off in technology stocks. However, the recent gain indicates a shift in investor sentiment, with SoftBank's shares now screening as undervalued with a current P/E of 7.0x compared to a fair ratio of 8.8x. Semiconductor firms Advantest and Tokyo Electron also posted gains, rising 2.65 percent and 1.76 percent respectively, contributing to the broader market recovery from the rout triggered by the Iran war. The rally in AI-related stocks has boosted investor confidence in SoftBank Group's investments.

stories.headline: MREIT books P1.25B distributable income in Q1 2026
stories.summary: MREIT Inc., the real estate investment trust of Megaworld Corp., reported a record P1.25-billion distributable income in the first quarter, up 34 percent year-on-year. The increase was driven by portfolio expansion, which added 34 percent to its gross leasable area, reaching 647,000 square meters. The expansion was applied retroactively to January 1, contributing to the income growth. Revenues also increased by 29 percent to P1.72 billion. The company's earnings growth outpaced revenue growth, reflecting stronger operating leverage as it scales up its office portfolio. MREIT is now embarking on its "Wave 5" acquisition, which is expected to mark its diversification into retail with the infusion of mall assets in the second half of the year, subject to due diligence, valuation, and regulatory approvals.

stories.headline: Filinvest RE unit to switch on 21-MWp solar facility in Misamis Oriental
stories.summary: FDC Misamis Power Corp., a unit of Filinvest Development Corp., is set to launch a 21-MWp solar facility in Misamis Oriental. The project is part of the company's efforts to expand its renewable energy portfolio. The solar facility will contribute to the region's power supply, supporting the company's goal of addressing the power deficiency in Mindanao. This initiative comes after the successful synchronization of the first generation unit of the company's 405-megawatt thermal power plant at the Phividec Industrial Estate to the Mindanao grid. The thermal power plant is a P30 billion investment, highlighting the company's commitment to providing much-needed power to the region. The launch of the solar facility marks another step towards achieving this objective.

stories.headline: SpaceX says it can buy AI coding tool Cursor for $60B later this year
stories.summary: SpaceX has announced an agreement to potentially acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year. Alternatively, the company may pay $10 billion for Cursor's work. This move is part of SpaceX's efforts to enhance its AI capabilities and compete with rivals Anthropic and OpenAI. Cursor develops code-writing software using artificial intelligence. The deal gives SpaceX the option to buy the startup at a later date. Recently, Cursor was reportedly seeking a $50 billion valuation in a private fundraising round. The potential acquisition is seen as a strategic move by SpaceX to advance its AI technology ahead of a planned Wall Street debut. The exact timing of the deal is undisclosed, but it is expected to take place later this year.

stories.headline: AI #165: In Our Image This was the week of Claude Opus 4.7. The reception was more mixed than usual.
stories.summary: Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic's latest AI model, was released this week, focusing on coding and complex tasks. The model's reception was mixed, with a notable blunder where it failed to solve a viral car wash puzzle. Claude Opus 4.7 is designed for tasks such as reasoning, planning, coding, image analysis, and internet search. It is available for free use without an account or login, and developers can access it via the Claude API. The model's pricing remains at $5/$25 per million tokens. Claude Opus 4.6, the previous version, is also available on major cloud platforms and requires advanced credits on HIX AI. The release of Claude Opus 4.7 has sparked interest and criticism, with many taking to the internet to discuss its capabilities and limitations. The model's performance will likely be closely watched in the coming weeks.

stories.headline: Elon Musk’s space exploration and AI company
stories.summary: SpaceX, led by Elon Musk, has acquired xAI, an artificial intelligence company also founded by Musk. The deal values xAI at $1.25 trillion. This move consolidates Musk's business interests, combining his space exploration and artificial intelligence ventures into a single company. The acquisition comes ahead of a expected massive initial public offering. xAI was founded by Musk three years ago, and its takeover by SpaceX is seen as an unconventional deal. The merger brings together two of Musk's privately held companies, streamlining his business operations. The deal is part of Musk's efforts to unify his various business interests, which also include other ventures beyond SpaceX and xAI.

stories.headline: SpaceX says it has the rights to buy artificial intelligence coding tool Cursor for $60 billion later this year
stories.summary: SpaceX has obtained the rights to buy artificial intelligence coding tool Cursor for $60 billion later this year. The deal gives SpaceX the option to acquire Cursor, a startup that makes code-writing software, at the specified price. Alternatively, SpaceX can pay $10 billion for the work they are doing together if it chooses not to exercise the acquisition option. This move is part of SpaceX's efforts to compete with rivals in AI coding. The agreement was announced by SpaceX, indicating the company's interest in expanding its capabilities in artificial intelligence and code-writing technology. The potential acquisition or partnership is expected to enhance SpaceX's position in the industry.
